topic.name: Renewable Energy and Emissions Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on legislative measures aimed at promoting renewable energy sources, regulating emissions, and improving air quality.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to modify the alcohol fuels tax credit and the alternative fuel tax credit
Sets forth sunset provisions terminating the small ethanol producer tax credit.
Document 2: Authorizes the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to designate areas nominated by local air pollution control districts as air and health quality empowerment zones
Eligible for grants for replacing or retrofitting polluting vehicles.
Document 3: Amends the Clean Air Act to make it unlawful to sell gasoline with less than 10% renewable fuel by volume.
Document 4: Requires EPA regulations to give states the option to impose a carbon tax for emissions.
Prohibits availability of the option to impose a carbon tax to states that are materially undermining the effectiveness of the tax.
Document 5: Requires the President to reduce semiannually the temporary duty imposed on ethanol.
Ensure that the combined temporary duty and any other duty imposed on ethanol is equal to, or lower than, any federal income or excise tax credit.
Document 6: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to impose a fee on fossil fuel products for carbon dioxide emissions.
Establishes the American Opportunity Trust Fund to provide economic assistance to low-income families.
Document 8: Revises the Corporate Average Fuel Economy (CAFE) standards by requiring at least a 4% annual increase in fuel economy.
Requires manufacturers to ensure that 50% of automobiles and light duty trucks manufactured in model years 2013-2014 are dual fueled.
Document 10: Amends the Energy Policy Act of 2005 to allow federally-guaranteed loans for renewable fuel pipeline construction.
Includes ethanol and biodiesel as renewable fuel.

topic.name: Environmental Conservation Legislation
topic.description: The documents focus on various legislative measures aimed at conserving natural resources and ecosystems in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to extend the award of remaining coral reef conservation program grant funds to appropriate projects
Authorizes actions to minimize injury to a coral reef or loss of an ecosystem function resulting from human activities
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to implement the Stipulation of Settlement dated September 13, 2006
Provides for the reintroduction of California Central Valley Spring Run Chinook salmon into the San Joaquin River
Document 4: Provides a categorical exclusion under the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 for a vegetation management project
to conserve or restore the habitat of the greater sage-grouse
Document 5: Authorizes the Secretary of the Army to carry out projects necessary to separate the hydrologic connection between the Great Lakes and Mississippi River basins
to prevent the interbasin transfer of aquatic invasive species
Document 6: Requires the Secretary of the Interior to promulgate regulations establishing a process for assessing the risk of all nonnative wildlife species proposed for importation into the United States
Establishes a Nonnative Wildlife Invasion Prevention Fund
Document 10: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow certain landowners whose property contains the habitat of an endangered or threatened species a tax credit for costs relating to habitat protection easements and restoration
Directs the Comptroller General of the United States to study and report to Congress on the effectiveness of such credit

topic.name: Medicare Policy Amendments
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various amendments and reforms related to the Medicare program under the Social Security Act.

Document 1: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to increase...
payment floor for direct graduate medical education (GME) payments under the Medicare program.
Document 2: This bill amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to prohibit payment...
study the treatment needs of individuals entitled to benefits under Medicare part A...
Document 3: Standard of Care Protection Act of 2013 - Declares that the development...
shall not be construed to establish the standard of care or duty of care owed by a health care provider to a patient...
Document 4: Puerto Rico Medicare Part B Equity Act of 2013 - Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act...
to repeal the exclusion of residents of Puerto Rico from deemed enrollment under part B.
Document 5: Medicare Ambulance Access Act of 2013 - Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act...
extend through December 31, 2018, certain temporary increases in rates...
Document 6: Small Business Health Care Relief Act of 2016 - Amends the Internal Revenue Code...
to exempt qualified small employer health reimbursement arrangements (HRAs) from certain requirements...
Document 7: Health Insurance Coverage Protection Act - Amends the Employee Retirement Income Security Act...
prohibit a group health plan from imposing an aggregate lifetime benefit limit...
Document 8: Colorectal Cancer Screening and Detection Coverage Age of 2009 - Amends the Public Health Service Act...
to require a group health plan to provide coverage for colorectal screening...
Document 9: Medicare Hospice Reform and Savings Act of 2009 - Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act...
revise payments and coverage for hospice care under the Medicare program.
Document 10: American Liberty Restoration Act - Repeals provisions of the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act...
that require individuals to maintain minimum essential health care coverage.

topic.name: Federal Legislative Protections
topic.description: This cluster discusses various federal legislative acts aimed at protecting specific rights and interests of individuals, including airline employees, students, servicemembers, property owners, and individuals involved in abortion-related issues.

Document 1: Requires an air carrier that furloughs an airline employee to retain such employee on a recall list for not less than 10 years after the furlough
Authorizes a furloughed employee to bring an action in U.S. district court to enforce this Act.
Document 2: Amends the National Voter Registration Act of 1993 to require states to designate federally-funded institutions of higher education as agencies for the registration of voters
Requires such schools to provide mail voter registration application forms to students registering to enroll in their courses.
Document 3: Amends the Servicemembers Civil Relief Act to require a court order to evict a servicemember from a primary residence during a period of the servicemember's military service
Prohibits a servicemember's failure or delay to make a mortgage payment from providing a basis for an adverse credit report.
Document 4: Allows an individual whose student loan debt is discharged due to undue hardship to recover the costs of the discharge proceeding
If the court finds that the position of the creditor was not substantially justified.
Document 5: Makes any state or political subdivision that carries out a public taking for any private purpose ineligible for any federal economic development funds for ten fiscal years
Entitles any owner of private property subject to such a taking to injunctive and declaratory relief.
Document 6: Imposes criminal penalties on anyone who knowingly performs an abortion based on the sex or gender of the child
Authorizes civil actions for verifiable money damages for injuries and punitive damages.
Document 7: Requires abortion providers to perform an obstetric ultrasound on the pregnant woman before she gives informed consent
Provides for civil penalties for willful failure to comply.
Document 8: Declares that nothing in the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act requires an individual to purchase health insurance coverage that includes coverage of abortion
Creates a cause of action for the Attorney General or any person adversely affected to obtain relief for violations of this prohibition.
Document 9: Declares that the right to life guaranteed by the Constitution is vested in each human being
Defines 'human being' to encompass all stages of life.
Document 10: Amends the federal judicial code to allow federal district courts to stay actions pending resolution of state law questions
Certifies the question to the appropriate court of that state.

topic.name: Housing Assistance and Support Programs
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative measures aimed at providing housing assistance, support for low-income families, and related social services.

Document 1: Rural Housing Preservation Act of 2016
extends rural housing vouchers to any low-income household
Document 6: Mortgage and Rental Assistance Reauthorization Act of 2009
authorize the President to provide temporary assistance in the form of mortgage or rental payments
Document 8: Housing Accountability Act of 2016
require an entity receiving low-income housing assistance payments to maintain decent, safe, and sanitary conditions
Document 9: Ensuring Child Care for Working Families Act of 2012
revise funding requirements for child care assistance to guarantee child care assistance for a family with a dependent child

topic.name: Federal Regulatory Reform
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative measures aimed at reforming federal regulations and improving transparency in government actions.

Document 1: improve the quality of the deliberations of Congress with respect to proposed federal mandates
enhance the ability of Congress and the public to identify federal mandates that may impose undue harm
Document 2: requires each federal agency to report to the Office of Information and Regulatory Affairs on the costs and benefits of each significant regulatory action
develop or have written procedures for the approval of significant guidance documents
Document 3: repeal the declaration that the deference that a court affords to the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau
determine the meaning or applicability of the terms of a CFPB agency action
Document 6: allows a small business operating in the United States to elect to be exempt from any federal rule or regulation
requires the small business to notify the federal agency that issued the rule or regulation
Document 9: requires a federal agency to include in each publication in the Federal Register pertaining to a rule the specific legal authority for the rule
file a report of each financial transaction required to be reported by a person under the Ethics in Government Act of 1978

topic.name: Data Privacy and Security Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative measures aimed at protecting personal data and ensuring privacy in different contexts, including employee benefits, educational settings, and telecommunications.

Document 1: amends the Internal Revenue Code and ERISA to treat employee benefit or pension plans maintained by Indian tribes
in the same manner as plans maintained by states
Document 2: Defines 'identity theft' to mean a fraud committed using the sensitive personal information of another individual
Requires any agency or person that owns or licenses computerized data containing sensitive personal information to develop, implement, and maintain reasonable security and notification procedures
Document 3: amends the federal criminal code to require a governmental entity operating a public unmanned aircraft system to minimize the collection or disclosure of covered information
prohibits a government entity from operating a public unmanned aircraft system and collecting or disclosing covered information for a law enforcement purpose
Document 4: Prohibits an operator of a school's Internet or online service from presenting students or their parents with targeted advertisements
Requires operators to implement information security procedures
Document 5: authorizes the FCC to impose forfeiture penalties without first sending a citation regarding the charged violation
expands prohibitions on the provision of inaccurate caller identification information
Document 8: Amends the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978 to modify the purposes for which the FBI is authorized to apply to a FISA court for an order requiring the production of tangible things
prohibits tangible things received by the FBI in response to a production order from being stored by a private entity

topic.name: Homeownership and Mortgage Assistance
topic.description: Legislative measures aimed at supporting homeownership and providing assistance to homeowners facing mortgage challenges.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) to carry out a pilot program to evaluate the effectiveness, in reducing defaults on residential mortgages.
Requires reduced premiums for mortgages secured by a 1- to 4-family dwelling for first-time homebuyers who complete a HUD-approved program of counseling.
Document 2: Amends the National Housing Act to extend the pilot program to establish an automated process for providing alternative credit rating information on mortgagors.
Addresses mortgagors with insufficient credit histories for determining their creditworthiness.
Document 3: Amends the Real Estate Settlement Procedures Act of 1974 to require servicers of federally related mortgage loans to establish a single electronic record accessible throughout the servicer.
Prescribes requirements for the affordable loan modification process, including evaluation of borrowers facing imminent default.
Document 4: Prohibits a covered mortgagee from initiating or continuing a foreclosure proceeding against a covered mortgagor unless eligibility for a qualified loan modification is determined.
Directs HUD to award grants to state housing finance agencies to assist eligible homeowners in satisfying any amounts past due on their home loans.
Document 7: Amends the Housing and Community Development Act of 1992 to modify requirements for annual reports by the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development on the activities of Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac.
Requires the enterprises to serve underserved markets and improve the distribution of investment capital available for mortgage financing.

topic.name: Energy Policy and Innovation
topic.description: The documents focus on legislative measures aimed at enhancing energy independence, innovation, and sustainability in the United States.

Document 1: Establishes the Commission on Energy Independence and Domestic Refining Capacity
Requires that such study examine the state of the domestic refining industry
Document 2: amends the Energy Policy Act of 2005 to revise the objectives for the civilian nuclear energy research and development programs
DOE shall carry out a specified program to enhance U.S. capabilities to develop new reactor technologies
Document 6: requires the Department of Energy (DOE) to expand its Industrial Assessment Centers program
provides assessment services to water and wastewater treatment facilities comparable to those for other energy efficiency improvements
Document 7: Establishes a national consumer product packaging reduction goal
Requires the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to develop and implement a plan to meet the national packaging reduction goal

topic.name: Agricultural Policy and Support
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative measures related to agricultural programs, support for farmers, and food safety regulations.

Document 1: extends specified commodity programs
provides assistance for livestock losses
Document 2: amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act
prohibits the use of federal funds for certain regulations
Document 4: prohibits Department of Commerce funds from being used for specified amendments
restricts gift parcels and humanitarian donations to Cuba
Document 6: amends the Federal Crop Insurance Act
makes available to crop producers additional coverage
Document 9: suspends specified agricultural price support authorities
regard to covered commodities, peanuts, sugar, and milk
Document 10: directs the Secretary of Agriculture to provide payments
for qualifying livestock disaster losses due to blizzard and drought

topic.name: Veterans and Seniors Benefits
topic.description: Legislation focused on enhancing benefits and protections for veterans and senior citizens in the United States.

Document 1: Guaranteed 3 Percent COLA for Seniors Act of 2015
require the use of such index to compute cost-of-living increases for Social Security benefits
Document 2: Sanctity of Eternal Rest for Veterans Act of 2011
prohibition on disruptions of funerals of members or former members of the Armed Forces
Document 3: Disabled Veterans Tax Termination Act
permit veterans with a service-connected disability of less than 50% to concurrently receive both retired pay and disability compensation
Document 4: increases in widow's and widower's insurance benefits by reason of delayed retirement
treatment of delayed retirement in cases of simultaneous entitlement to OASDI benefits
Document 5: Charlie Wolf Nuclear Workers Compensation Act
expand the diseases for which individuals who were exposed to radiation from nuclear testing may claim compensation
Document 6: authorize monthly annuity payments under the Survivor Benefit Plan
for the sole benefit of a disabled dependent child who is incapable of self-support
Document 7: Gold Star Fathers Act of 2011
includes as a preference eligible for federal employment purposes a parent of an individual who lost his or her life under honorable conditions
Document 8: Extended Unemployment Benefits Reform Act of 2012
prohibit state payment of extended UC to an individual for any week in which he or she does not perform at least 20 hours of unpaid public service
Document 9: Mental Health Exposure Military Official Record Act of 2014
requires the Secretary of Defense to establish a significant event tracker system
Document 10: Expediting Veteran's Benefits Act
extend Department of Veterans Affairs authority for the performance of medical disability evaluations by contract physicians

topic.name: Health Care Workforce and Services Improvement
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative efforts aimed at improving health care services, workforce training, and patient safety across various health care sectors.

Document 1: requires the Department of Labor to establish a standard on safe patient handling, mobility, and injury prevention
requires health care employers to develop and implement a safe patient handling program
Document 2: requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to review uptake and utilization of diabetes screening benefits
requires the Secretary to establish an advisory group to examine and recommend best practices of employee wellness
Document 3: requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to make grants to integrate mental health services for older adults into primary care
requires state plans for community mental health services grants to describe services for older individuals
Document 4: amends the Social Security Act to increase the type of originating sites allowed to offer telehealth services
authorizes grants to demonstrate how telehealth technologies can be used to improve health care services
Document 5: directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services to establish a demonstration program to facilitate physician reentry into primary care
requires the Secretary to assess the need for additional primary care physicians
Document 6: requires the Secretary of Labor to promulgate a standard on safe patient handling to prevent musculoskeletal disorders
includes requirements for health care employers to train their workers on safe patient handling
Document 7: expands the TRICARE health care program to entitle additional female beneficiaries to pregnancy prevention care
prohibits cost-sharing for pregnancy prevention care
Document 8: requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to establish a program for the development of an integrated nutrition curriculum
addresses topics such as nutrition across the life cycle and food insecurity
Document 9: requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to establish a hereditary hemorrhagic telangiectasia initiative
directs the Secretary to establish the HHT Coordinating Committee to advance research
Document 10: directs the Department of Veterans Affairs to carry out a Docs-to-Doctors Program to recruit health care individuals
provides full practice authority to advanced practice registered nurses and physician assistants

topic.name: Education Access and Support Programs
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at improving access to education and support services for students, particularly those from underrepresented or disadvantaged backgrounds.

Document 1: Authorizes the Secretary of Education to award matching six-year grants to local educational agencies (LEAs) that partner with institutions of higher education (IHEs) to establish or support dual enrollment programs.
Gives grant priority to applicants that propose to establish or support a dual enrollment program for a student body at least 40% of which is impoverished.
Document 2: The Scholarships for Opportunity and Results Act (SOAR) is amended to limit its focus to students in the lowest-performing Washington, DC, elementary and secondary schools.
Priorities for the award of scholarships are modified to give priority to students who in the preceding school year attended a low-achieving elementary or secondary school.
Document 3: This bill amends the Carl D. Perkins Career and Technical Education Act of 2006 to modify the requirements for career and technical education programs to align the programs with adult education programs.
States may develop core indicators of performance for career and technical education students at the adult education level.
Document 4: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to require the Secretary of Education to make matching grants of at least $1 million to states for allocation to local educational agencies (LEAs) so that additional school-based mental health and student service providers may be hired.
Sets those minimum ratios at: 1 school counselor for every 250 students.
Document 5: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to allow states to require the use of adaptive assessments of student progress toward state academic performance standards.
Defines an 'adaptive assessment' as one that changes in difficulty in accordance with a student's performance.
Document 6: This bill amends title III (Institutional Aid) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to allow historically black colleges and universities and other minority-serving institutions of higher education to use title III funds to establish resource centers on campus that provide support to and education about lesbian, gay, bisexual, transgender, and queer students.
Document 7: Deems a program of education as an approved program for purposes of the post-9/11 veterans' educational assistance program if the program is offered by an institution offering postsecondary instruction that leads to an associate or higher degree.
Instruction that does not lead to an associate or higher degree and the institution is an approved educational institution.
Document 8: Authorizes the Secretary of Education to award matching six-year grants to local educational agencies (LEAs) that partner with institutions of higher education (IHEs) to establish or support dual enrollment programs.
Requires applicants to make assurances that students will not be required to pay tuition or fees for postsecondary courses.
Document 9: Amends the Child Nutrition Act of 1966 to require the Secretary of Agriculture to award competitive grants to state or local educational agencies (LEAs) for the establishment or enhancement of school breakfast programs.
Gives priority to subgrant applicant schools at least 75% of whose students are eligible for free or reduced-price school lunches.
Document 10: Directs the Secretary of Education to allocate grants to states and, through them, subgrants to local educational agencies (LEAs) for the costs of retaining, recalling, rehiring, or hiring employees to provide early childhood, elementary, or secondary education and related services.
Prohibits the use of such grants to supplant state funding for education.

topic.name: Land Management and Tribal Recognition
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to land management, transfers, and the recognition of tribal entities in the United States.

Document 1: transfers...to a specified parcel of land
reversionary interest of the United States
Document 2: expedited review of the petition
recognition as a federal Indian tribe
Document 3: convey...to the city of Tucson
unencumbering the title to such land
Document 4: identifies roadless areas
manage such Areas to maintain their roadless character
Document 5: extends federal recognition to the Little Shell Tribe
eligible for all services and benefits
Document 6: replaces the map...of the Coastal Barrier Resources System
relating to Unit P17 in the state of Florida
Document 7: sell and quitclaim all interest of the United States
parcel of real property...in Riverside, California
Document 8: designates certain lands...as the Wild Sky Wilderness
managed by the Secretary of Agriculture
Document 9: directs the Secretary of Agriculture...to set an annual fee
payment of a transfer fee to the United States
Document 10: authorizes the Department of the Interior to retire any coal preference right lease application
designates approximately 7,242 acres of land as the Ah-shi-sle-pah Wilderness

topic.name: Small Business Support Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various legislative measures aimed at supporting and enhancing the capabilities of small businesses in the United States.

Document 1: Building Better Business Partnerships Act of 2012 - Amends the Small Business Act to authorize the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) to establish a mentor-protege program for all small businesses.

Prohibits a federal department or agency from carrying out a mentor-protege program for small businesses unless the head of such department or agency submits a program plan to the Administrator and the Administrator approves it.
Document 2: Science and Technology Innovation Act of 2008 - Amends the Small Business Act to: (1) extend through FY2010 the Small Business Innovation Research (SBIR) and Small Business Technology Transfer (STTR) programs;
increase SBIR and STTR set-asides and award levels.
Document 3: Small Business Development Centers Relief Act of 2010 - Directs the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) to waive the requirement that a matching amount be provided as a condition of grants authorized under the SBA's small business development center program.
Document 4: Requires at least 10% of funds made available for high-speed rail corridor planning and development projects to be expended through small business concerns owned and controlled by socially and economically disadvantaged individuals.
Expresses the sense of Congress that the federal government should continue to encourage participation by veteran-owned small businesses in federal contracting and procurement opportunities.
Document 5: Ports as Small Business Incubators Act of 2012 - Amends the Small Business Act to direct the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) to establish the Ports as Small Business Incubators Program.
Requires each Program participant, in providing such access, to give a priority to small businesses owned and controlled by women, veterans, or socially and economically disadvantaged individuals.
Document 6: Small Business Administration Franchise Loan Transparency Act of 2015 - This bill requires a franchisor to disclose in the required Federal Trade Commission disclosure document.

Any financial information relating to the performance of any franchise's location provided by the franchisor, or its representatives, to the lender for the purpose of qualifying the loan, must be disclosed to the prospective franchisee borrower.
Document 7: Military Reservist and Veteran Small Business Reauthorization and Opportunity Act of 2007 - Increases funding for the Office of Veterans Business Development of the Small Business Administration (SBA).

Directs the President to establish an interagency task force to coordinate federal efforts to increase capital and business development opportunities for small businesses owned and controlled by service-disabled veterans and veterans.
Document 8: Small Business Financing and Investment Act of 2009 - Amends the Small Business Act to direct the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) to establish and carry out a program to provide support to SBA regional, district, and branch offices.

Authorizes the Administrator to carry out a Community Express Program of loans of up to $250,000 to small businesses the majority ownership of which is held by women, members of qualified Indian tribes, socially or economically disadvantaged individuals, veterans, or members of reserve components of the Armed Forces.
Document 9: Assuring Contracting Equity Act of 2015 - This bill increases from 23% to 25% the government-wide small business procurement contract goal.

The Small Business Administration (SBA) must include in its annual report to the President and Congress on these goals the percentage of participation by small businesses for the total value of all prime contracts and subcontracts awards for a fiscal year.
Document 10: Small Business Administration Express Loan Extension Act of 2011 - Amends the Small Business Jobs Act of 2010 to extend through September 27, 2012 a temporary increase from $350,000 to $1 million in the maximum loan amount under the Express Loan Program of the Small Business Administration (SBA).

topic.name: Immigration and National Security Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various legislative measures related to immigration policies and national security enhancements in the United States.

Document 1: Telecommuters Tax Fairness Act of 2007 - Prohibits a state from imposing an income tax on the compensation of a nonresident individual for any period in which such individual is not physically present in or working in such state.
Prohibits deeming such nonresident individual to be present in or working in such state on the grounds that such individual's work at home fails any convenience of the employer test.
Document 2: Expresses the sense of Congress that the United States should improve homeland security by strengthening the security of the visa waiver program.
Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act with respect to the visa waiver program to provide that the Secretary of Homeland Security shall certify to Congress that an air exit system is in place.
Document 3: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to create an E-4 treaty trader visa category for up to 15,000 nationals of the Republic of Korea.
The Secretary of Labor has certified to the Secretary of Homeland Security that the intending employer has filed an attestation concerning U.S. worker protections.
Document 6: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act (INA) to require that visa and admissions petitions and applications filed with the Department of Homeland Security must contain all required signatures.
A background check shall include a review of social media activity.
Document 9: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act regarding the visa waiver program to prohibit a national of a program country who has traveled to Iraq or Syria from traveling to the United States without a visa.

The Department of Homeland Security may prohibit a national of a program country from traveling to the United States if the national has traveled to a country in which a designated foreign terrorist organization has a significant presence.

topic.name: Criminal Justice Legislation
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts aimed at amending federal criminal laws to address issues related to crime, public safety, and accountability.

Document 1: Amends the federal criminal code to expand prohibitions against criminal street gangs.
Imposes criminal penalties on individuals who knowingly commit, or conspire, threaten, or attempt to commit, a gang crime.
Document 2: This bill amends the federal criminal code to make it a crime to use the mail or any facility or means of interstate commerce to knowingly publish personally identifiable information.
A violator is subject to criminal penalties—a fine, a prison term of up to five years, or both.
Document 3: Requires that the criminal background check of military child care system employees be conducted pursuant to regulations prescribed by the Department of Defense.
Includes searches of state criminal and sex offender registries and child abuse and neglect registries.
Document 4: Amends the federal criminal code to impose a fine and/or a prison term of up to two years on any person who travels in interstate or foreign commerce to evade compliance with a court-ordered property distribution.
Requires payment of restitution equal to the total unpaid property distribution from any person convicted of evading payment.
Document 5: Amends the federal criminal code to authorize the Attorney General to deny the transfer of a firearm or the issuance of a firearms or explosives license.
Imposes criminal penalties on individuals engaged in terrorist activities who smuggle or knowingly bring firearms into the United States.
Document 6: Amends the Violence Against Women Act of 2000 to require the Attorney General to include within the national protocol on sexual assault forensic examinations a recommendation that a victim of sexual assault who is at risk of pregnancy from rape should be offered information about emergency contraception.
Requires the Attorney General to revise any existing protocol in accordance with this Act.
Document 7: Directs the Attorney General to expand resources for the Project Gunrunner initiative to identify, investigate, and prosecute firearms trafficking across the U.S.-Mexico border.
Provide ATF agents, equipment, and training to assist Mexican law enforcement officers in combating firearms trafficking.
Document 8: Amends the federal criminal code to include as a fraud offense a scheme or artifice by a public official to engage in undisclosed self-dealing.
Defines 'undisclosed self-dealing' to mean performing an official act to benefit or further a financial interest of such public official.
Document 9: Amends the Controlled Substances Act to eliminate increased and mandatory minimum penalties for drug offenses involving mixtures or substances which contain cocaine base.
Authorizes appropriations for federal pretrial diversion and post conviction drug courts.
Document 10: Amends the federal criminal code to revise the civil penalties for violations of firearms law and the procedures for assessing such penalties.
Requires the Attorney General to make preliminary determinations on firearms license applications.

topic.name: Unemployment and Social Security Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on unemployment benefits, social security reforms, and related economic assistance programs.

Document 1: Amends the Social Security Act to require the Secretary of Labor to make unemployment compensation modernization incentive payments
Limits the use of transferred amounts to the payment of cash unemployment benefits
Document 3: Extends the final date for entering a federal-state agreement under the Emergency Unemployment Compensation program
Postpones the termination of the program until June 9, 2012
Document 5: Authorizes the Secretary of the Treasury to issue U.S. obligations in an amount equal to the monthly insurance benefits payable under title II of the Social Security Act
Document 6: Amends the Congressional Budget Act of 1974 to provide a point of order against consideration of any budget resolution that sets forth totals for any fiscal year with respect to the Social Security Trust Funds
Defines 'Social Security reform legislation' as a bill or joint resolution to save Social Security

topic.name: Government Accountability and Transparency
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing accountability, transparency, and data management within federal agencies and programs.

1: require state Medicaid plans to provide that the state will establish and maintain laws to require disclosure of information on hospital charges
provide individuals with information about estimated out-of-pocket costs for health care services
2: establish policies, procedures, and standards for agencies to follow in the event of a breach of data security
timely notification to individuals whose sensitive personal information could be compromised
3: preservation by the Department of Defense (DOD) of documentary evidence of incidents of sexual assault or harassment
full protection of the victim's privacy and lifetime access to such evidence
4: require the electronic capture, management, and preservation of such electronic records in accordance with the Federal Records Act
establish mandatory minimum functional requirements for electronic records management systems
5: establish a systematic method for assessing the effectiveness and accountability of agency programs
evaluate executive agencies and their programs identified in each unit identified in the assessment
6: expand the functions of the Privacy and Civil Liberties Oversight Board to include reviews of legislation, regulations, policies
investigate whistleblower complaints related to privacy or civil liberties concerns
7: assess redundant reporting requirements imposed by the Administrator on state, local, and tribal governments
develop a set of quantifiable performance measures and metrics to assess the effectiveness of the programs
8: revise or prescribe requirements for greater disclosure and electronic filing of personal financial information by Members of Congress
make each financial quarterly report filed by a Member available within 48 hours after its receipt
9: conduct a study of gaps in U.S. port security and submit to Congress a report
share relevant information with designated points of contact from federal agencies, state, local, or tribal governments
10: establish a Vote by Mail program of grants to participating states
evaluate and report to Congress on the benefits of broader implementation of mail-in voting

topic.name: Military and Defense Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts focused on military personnel, defense policies, and national security measures.

Document 1: Military Spouse Employment Programs Evaluation Act
review all current Department of Defense (DOD) military spouse employment programs
Document 2: Gender Equality Combat Act
termination of the ground combat exclusion policy for female members of the Armed Forces
Document 3: National Nuclear Security Administration Accountability Act
subject to the authority, direction, and control of the Secretary of Energy
Document 4: Prohibits any funds made available to the Department of Defense
increase the number of U.S. Armed Forces serving in Iraq
Document 5: DoD Suicide Tracking Act of 2014
policy for the development of a standard method for collecting, reporting, and assessing suicide data
Document 6: Servicemembers' Zika Protection Act of 2016
DOD funds available for the deployment of insect repellant
Document 7: Aegis Ashore Test Facility Construction Authorization Act
authorize a military construction project at the Hawaii Pacific Missile Range Facility
Document 8: No More Ghost Money Act
prohibits any federal department from making a monetary payment to any official of a foreign country
Document 9: Protecting Americans from the Proliferation of Weapons to Terrorists Act of 2013
prohibits the obligation or expenditure of funds for military operations in Syria
Document 10: Intelligence Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2008
authorizes appropriations for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities

topic.name: Tariff Suspension Amendments
topic.description: The documents discuss temporary suspensions and reductions of duties on various goods in the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States.

Document 1: suspend temporarily the duty on certain woven fabrics
Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 2: reduce temporarily the duty on certain sports footwear
Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 3: suspend temporarily the duty on certain leathered footwear
Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 4: extend the temporary suspension of duty on poly(toluene diisocyanate)
Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 5: suspend temporarily the duty on oxirane, 2-methyl-
Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States

topic.name: Food and Drug Safety Regulations
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative measures aimed at ensuring the safety and regulation of food, drugs, and related practices in the United States.

Document 1: Preservation of Antibiotics for Medical Treatment Act of 2011
requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to deny an application for a new animal drug that is a critical antimicrobial animal drug unless...
Document 2: Safe and Accurate Food Labeling Act of 2015
requires the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) to continue the voluntary consultation process established under the FDA’s 'Statement of Policy: Foods Derived from New Plant Varieties.'
Document 4: Food and Drug Administration Amendments Act of 2007
amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (FFDCA) to include postmarket safety activities within the process for the review of human drug applications or supplements.

topic.name: Maritime and Energy Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on U.S. legislation related to maritime transportation, energy production, and environmental regulations concerning oil and gas exploration.

Document 1: promote the transportation of exports of liquefied natural gas from the United States
processing of a license for liquefied natural gas facilities at a deepwater port
Document 2: repeal the limitation on the liability of the party responsible for an offshore facility
oil is discharged into or upon navigable waters
Document 3: construction of the Rails for Resources project in Alaska
Document 4: establish and implement enforceable uniform national standards for the regulation of discharges incidental to the normal operation of a vessel
issue a final rule for the regulation of ballast water discharges
Document 5: prohibits the Secretary of the Interior from issuing a lease for oil exploration or extraction from specified submerged Outer Continental Shelf lands
Document 6: allow tax credits for investment in an offshore facility using wind to produce electricity
facility located in the coastal waters of the United States
Document 7: amends the Safe Drinking Water Act to repeal the exemption from restrictions on underground injection of fluids
requires disclosure of chemicals and proppants intended for use in hydraulic fracturing
Document 8: prohibit the Secretary of the Interior from offering any tract for oil and gas leasing
Eastern Gulf of Mexico and the Straits of Florida Planning Area
Document 9: require the Secretary to approve an oil spill response plan
prohibit an exploration plan from being eligible for a categorical exclusion under NEPA
Document 10: conduct a lease sale in each outer Continental Shelf planning area
repeal the prohibition against leasing or other development leading to production of oil and gas from the Arctic National Wildlife Refuge

topic.name: Student Loan Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at reforming student loan policies and financial aid processes in higher education.

Document 1: excludes from the gross income of an individual the discharge of student loans due to the death or disability of the student
require the Department of Education to discharge the liability on loans that parents received on behalf of a student who has become permanently and totally disabled
Document 2: amends the Higher Education Opportunity Act to refer to the Secretary of Education's model institution of higher education financial aid offer form as the standard form
requires the standard form to include certain additional items
Document 6: direct the Secretary of Education to include a space on the Free Application for Federal Student Aid (FAFSA) form to identify students who are or were in the foster care system
notify students who identify themselves as such of their potential eligibility for financial aid
Document 10: require institutions of higher education participating in the William D. Ford Federal Direct Loan program to accept specified risk-sharing requirements
must remit a risk-sharing payment that declines as the cohort default rate declines

topic.name: Tax Code Amendments
topic.description: This cluster discusses various amendments to the Internal Revenue Code aimed at modifying tax regulations and benefits for individuals and corporations.

Document 1: Timber Revitalization and Economic Enhancement Act of 2009
reduction in the rate of tax on the net timber gain of corporations
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code
allow a five-year carryback of net operating losses
Document 3: taxpayer election to treat combat zone compensation as earned income
calculating earned income tax credit amounts
Document 4: Keeping Our Social Security Promises Act
apply employment and self-employment taxes
Document 5: tax deduction for charitable contributions of food inventory
increase from 10% to 15% of taxpayer net income
Document 6: Dave Thomas Adoption Act of 2013
penalty-free withdrawals from individual retirement accounts for adoption expenses
Document 7: Achieving a Better Life Experience Act of 2011
establish tax-exempt ABLE accounts for individuals with disabilities
Document 8: Freight Rail Infrastructure Capacity Expansion Act of 2010
tax credit for 25% of the cost of new qualified freight rail infrastructure property
Document 9: amends title XIX (Medicaid)
treat qualified lottery winnings and lump sum income for determining eligibility
Document 10: Legal Services Benefit Act of 2009
exclusion from gross income for amounts received under qualified group legal services plans
